description: Platform Modernization - Cross-platform CMake build, modular source layout, SQLite persistence, JSON REST API, React+TypeScript web GUI

userStories.title: CMake build system with vcpkg integration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a CMake build system with vcpkg so that MeOS builds on any platform.
acceptanceCriteria: Top-level CMakeLists.txt exists and builds a minimal executable (main.cpp with empty main)
vcpkg.json manifest declares initial dependencies (nlohmann-json, sqlite3)
Build succeeds on Linux with GCC
userStories.notes: CMake and vcpkg integrated with support for Debug and Release via presets.

userStories.title: Modular source directory structure
userStories.description: As a developer, I want source code organized in logical modules under src/ with CMake targets per module.
acceptanceCriteria: src/ directory created with subdirectories: app/, domain/, net/, db/, util/, io/
CMake targets defined per module as static libraries
Header include paths configured so bare #include works within modules
Main executable links all module libraries
Build succeeds with empty module libraries
userStories.notes: Created app, domain, net, db, util, io modules as static libraries and linked them to the meos executable.

userStories.title: Google Test integration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Google Test set up via vcpkg so that I can write and run unit tests with ctest.
acceptanceCriteria: Google Test added to vcpkg.json dependencies
CMake test target configured with ctest support
At least one sample test compiles and passes
userStories.notes: Google Test integrated via vcpkg and ctest. Added sample test and documentation.

userStories.title: GitHub Actions CI pipeline
userStories.description: As a developer, I want CI that builds and tests on every push to main and on pull requests.
acceptanceCriteria: GitHub Actions workflow file exists at .github/workflows/ci.yml
Workflow builds on Linux (GCC) and Windows (MSVC)
Workflow triggers on push to main and on pull requests
Build artifacts uploaded for Linux and Windows
userStories.notes: Created GitHub Actions CI pipeline supporting Linux and Windows with GTest and vcpkg integration.

userStories.title: Migrate core utilities to src/util/
userStories.description: As a developer, I want meos_util, meosException, and string conversion utilities migrated so other modules can use them.
acceptanceCriteria: Wide string / narrow string conversion utilities (string2Wide etc.) preserved and working
Utility library compiles as static library on Linux
userStories.notes: Migrated meos_util and meosexception to src/util/ with cross-platform implementations for Linux.

userStories.title: Migrate parser and time utilities to src/util/
userStories.description: As a developer, I want XML/CSV parsers and time utilities migrated to src/util/.
acceptanceCriteria: All migrated code compiles on Linux without Win32 dependencies

userStories.title: Migrate localization system to src/util/
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the localization system migrated to support internationalized strings cross-platform.
acceptanceCriteria: Language loading and string lookup work correctly
No Win32-specific API calls in localizer

userStories.title: Migrate domain base classes to src/domain/
userStories.description: As a developer, I want oBase and oDataContainer migrated as the foundation for all domain entities.
acceptanceCriteria: Domain library compiles with dependency on util library only

userStories.title: Migrate control and punch entities to src/domain/
userStories.description: As a developer, I want oControl and oPunch migrated as leaf entities with few dependencies.
acceptanceCriteria: Control types and validation logic preserved

userStories.title: Migrate course entity to src/domain/
userStories.description: As a developer, I want oCourse migrated with its control sequence logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Course length and climb data preserved

userStories.title: Migrate club and card entities to src/domain/
userStories.description: As a developer, I want oClub, oCard, and oFreePunch migrated to src/domain/.

userStories.title: Migrate runner entity to src/domain/
userStories.description: As a developer, I want oAbstractRunner and oRunner migrated with result computation logic.
acceptanceCriteria: oRunner.h/cpp migrated to src/domain/ (includes oAbstractRunner base)

userStories.title: Migrate team and class entities to src/domain/
userStories.description: As a developer, I want oTeam and oClass migrated with relay and qualification logic.

userStories.title: Migrate event aggregate root to src/domain/
userStories.description: As a developer, I want oEvent migrated as the root that owns all domain entity collections.
acceptanceCriteria: Event owns collections of all domain entities
No direct database or GUI dependencies in domain layer

userStories.title: Migrate GeneralResult to src/domain/
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the pluggable result computation system migrated to src/domain/.
acceptanceCriteria: Strategy pattern for scoring algorithms preserved
Result computation works independently of GUI

userStories.title: Domain layer unit tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want unit tests for core domain operations to verify correctness.
acceptanceCriteria: At least one test per domain entity (oRunner, oClass, oCourse, oControl, oClub, oCard, oTeam)
Tests cover creation, ID assignment, and basic property access

userStories.title: Database abstraction interface
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a database interface in src/db/ so persistence is decoupled from the domain.
acceptanceCriteria: Abstract database interface defined in src/db/ as a C++ abstract class
Interface defines CRUD methods for all domain entities
Interface is implementation-agnostic (no SQLite-specific types exposed)
Domain layer depends on interface only, not implementation

userStories.title: SQLite schema implementation
userStories.description: As a developer, I want SQLite tables created for all domain entities.
acceptanceCriteria: SQLite implementation of database interface in src/db/
Schema covers: competitions, runners, teams, classes, clubs, courses, controls, cards, punches
Schema creation and migration handled on startup
SQLite database stored as single file in working directory

userStories.title: SQLite CRUD operations
userStories.description: As a developer, I want full CRUD operations for all entities via SQLite.
acceptanceCriteria: Create, read, update, delete operations implemented for all domain entities
List operations with basic filtering support
Concurrent access handled with WAL mode
Unit tests for CRUD operations pass

userStories.title: HTTP server setup and API framework
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an HTTP server in src/net/ that serves REST endpoints with JSON.
acceptanceCriteria: HTTP server library integrated (cpp-httplib or restbed via vcpkg)
Server starts and listens on configurable port
Consistent error response format with error codes

userStories.title: Competition and class API endpoints
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I want CRUD endpoints for competitions and classes.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/v1/classes/{id} returns single class with course assignment
Input validation returns meaningful error messages

userStories.title: Runner and team API endpoints
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I want CRUD endpoints for runners and teams.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/v1/runners supports filtering by name, club, class

userStories.title: Course, control, and club API endpoints
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I want CRUD endpoints for courses, controls, and clubs.

userStories.title: Results and startlist API endpoints
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I want API endpoints for computed results and start lists.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/v1/results returns computed results per class
GET /api/v1/startlist returns start lists per class
Result computation uses existing GeneralResult logic
Support for both preliminary and final results
POST /api/v1/runners/{id}/status allows manual status changes (DNS, DNF, DSQ)

userStories.title: Card readout API endpoint
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I want an endpoint to submit card readout data.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/v1/cards accepts card readout data in JSON format
Card data is matched to runner and punches are recorded
Response includes matched runner and computed status
Validation errors returned for malformed card data

userStories.title: React + TypeScript project setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a React 18+ TypeScript project initialized with Vite in src/ui/web/.
acceptanceCriteria: React 18+ app with TypeScript in src/ui/web/
Dev server runs and shows placeholder page

userStories.title: App shell with routing and navigation
userStories.description: As a user, I want a navigation layout with tabs mirroring the existing MeOS tab structure.
acceptanceCriteria: Navigation sidebar or tab bar with links to all routes
Layout has header with app name and content area
Responsive design works on tablet-sized screens

userStories.title: API client layer with typed interfaces
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a typed API client so frontend components can call the REST API with type safety.
acceptanceCriteria: TypeScript interfaces for all domain entities (Runner, Team, Class, Club, Course, Control, Card)
API client module with functions for all CRUD operations
Error handling with typed error responses
Base URL configurable for dev proxy and production

userStories.title: Competition management page
userStories.description: As a competition organizer, I want to create and configure competitions via the web interface.
acceptanceCriteria: Create new competition form with name, date, organizer fields
Form validation with user-friendly error messages

userStories.title: Classes management page
userStories.description: As a competition organizer, I want to manage classes through the web interface.
acceptanceCriteria: Table view listing all classes with name, course, start method

userStories.title: Courses management page
userStories.description: As a competition organizer, I want to manage courses through the web interface.
acceptanceCriteria: Table view listing all courses with name, length, number of controls
Add new course form with control sequence editor

userStories.title: Clubs and controls management pages
userStories.description: As a competition organizer, I want to manage clubs and controls through the web interface.
acceptanceCriteria: Table view listing all clubs with name
Table view listing all controls with code and type
Both tables support sorting and filtering

userStories.title: Runners management page
userStories.description: As a competition organizer, I want to manage runners through the web interface.
acceptanceCriteria: Table view listing runners with name, club, class, start time, card number
Add new runner form with club and class dropdowns

userStories.title: Teams management page
userStories.description: As a competition organizer, I want to manage teams through the web interface.
acceptanceCriteria: Table view listing teams with name, club, class
Add new team form with member assignment
Edit team details and manage team members

userStories.title: Runner import functionality
userStories.description: As a competition organizer, I want to import runners from CSV and IOF XML files.
acceptanceCriteria: File upload component for CSV and IOF XML files
Import creates runners with club and class assignments
Error handling for malformed files with user-friendly messages

userStories.title: Results view page
userStories.description: As a competition organizer, I want to view results per class with split times.
acceptanceCriteria: Results view with class selector dropdown
Results table showing position, name, club, time, status
Auto-refresh for live results via polling
Print-friendly formatting via CSS print styles

userStories.title: Start list view page
userStories.description: As a competition organizer, I want to view start lists per class.
acceptanceCriteria: Start list view with class selector dropdown
Start list table showing start time, name, club, card number
Print-friendly formatting via CSS print styles

userStories.title: Results export functionality
userStories.description: As a competition organizer, I want to export results in CSV and IOF XML formats.
acceptanceCriteria: Export button on results page with format selection
CSV export with all result fields

userStories.title: Frontend test setup with Vitest
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Vitest configured with React Testing Library and MSW for frontend tests.
acceptanceCriteria: React Testing Library installed and configured
MSW (Mock Service Worker) set up for API mocking
At least one component test passes
npm test runs all frontend tests successfully

userStories.title: Static file serving from C++ server
userStories.description: As a user, I want the C++ executable to serve the React frontend so no separate web server is needed.
acceptanceCriteria: C++ HTTP server serves static files from bundled directory
index.html served at / with SPA fallback (non-API routes return index.html)

userStories.title: CMake integration for frontend build
userStories.description: As a developer, I want CMake to build the React frontend as part of the full build.
acceptanceCriteria: CMake runs npm install and npm run build for src/ui/web/
React production build output copied to server static files directory
Full build produces single distributable binary with bundled frontend
Build succeeds on Linux and Windows

userStories.title: IOF XML import/export migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want IOF 3.0 XML import/export migrated to src/io/.
acceptanceCriteria: IOF XML import produces correct domain objects
IOF XML export generates valid IOF 3.0 XML
No Win32 dependencies in I/O code

userStories.title: CSV and HTML export migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want CSV import/export and HTML result generation migrated to src/io/.
acceptanceCriteria: HTML result generation works without Win32 dependencies

userStories.title: PDF generation migration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want PDF generation via libharu migrated to src/io/.
acceptanceCriteria: PDF generation produces valid PDF files

userStories.title: Remove Win32 GUI code
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Win32-specific GUI code removed for full platform independence.
acceptanceCriteria: All Tab*.cpp/h files removed or archived
gdioutput.cpp/h and related GDI files removed
No Windows-only headers (windows.h, commctrl.h) in domain or server code
Application compiles and runs on Linux without Win32 dependencies

userStories.title: CI linting integration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want linting in CI for code quality enforcement.
acceptanceCriteria: clang-tidy configured for C++ source files
ESLint runs on TypeScript files in CI
